skill.name: flow-nexus-neural
skill.description: Guide Flow Nexus MCP workflows for neural network training, inference, distributed clusters, templates, and model management.
skill.summary: Train, deploy, monitor, and manage neural networks through Flow Nexus MCP tools.
skill.author: ruvnet

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command detections, hardcoded URLs, weak cryptography matches, and system reconnaissance matches. Review found the Ruby backtick, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance detections are false positives caused by Markdown fences, JavaScript MCP examples, and normal neural network text. The material risk is user-directed installation of an npm MCP server and authenticated remote compute operations; no prompt injection attempt was found.

medium_findings.title: User-Directed Remote Package Execution
medium_findings.description: The prerequisites instruct users to add a Flow Nexus MCP server and run npx commands using the latest package version. This is legitimate setup for the skill, but it executes code from an external package source and should be pinned or reviewed before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit setup steps that execute a package through npx. They are documented user actions rather than hidden execution, so the risk is elevated but not malicious.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Remote Compute Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill guides users through creating distributed training clusters, deploying worker nodes, running training jobs, and publishing marketplace templates. These operations can consume external resources or publish artifacts, so users need clear consent and account scope controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file repeatedly documents MCP calls that deploy nodes, run training, and publish or rate templates. The actions match the stated purpose and require authentication, which supports a medium operational risk rather than a malicious finding.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External Service URLs
low_findings.description: The resource section links to Flow Nexus documentation, templates, API reference, and registration. These URLs are expected for a Flow Nexus skill and do not show data exfiltration or hidden callbacks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation and registration links in the resources section. No evidence found that they transmit secrets or collect local data.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations occur in front matter, normal descriptions, or neural network examples. No evidence found of cryptographic functions, hashing APIs, encryption routines, or credential handling at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review of the flagged lines showed prose and machine learning examples, not crypto code. The scanner likely matched substrings inside ordinary words and model configuration text.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported system reconnaissance locations describe neural cluster architecture and training job status. No evidence found of host enumeration commands, local system probing, or environment inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are fields inside documented MCP examples for model architecture and training status. They do not query the local machine or collect system information.

dangerous_patterns.title: Latest-Version npx Installation Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup command uses npx with the latest Flow Nexus package. This pattern can change behavior over time and should be reviewed or pinned for controlled environments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is visible and intentional. It is not hidden, but latest-version package execution is a known supply chain risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Authenticated MCP Operations
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses Flow Nexus MCP calls for cluster creation, training, inference, and marketplace actions. These calls operate outside the local workspace and should require explicit user approval.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples clearly invoke external MCP tools for remote compute and publishing workflows. They are aligned with the skill purpose, so the concern is operational control rather than malicious intent.

content.user_title: Train Neural Models with Flow Nexus
content.value_statement: Neural training often needs repeatable setup, remote execution, and model monitoring. This skill guides Flow Nexus MCP workflows for training, inference, distributed clusters, templates, and benchmarking.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to train feedforward, LSTM, GAN, autoencoder, and transformer models.
Guides model inference with trained model identifiers and batch inputs.
Explains template discovery, deployment, publishing, and rating workflows.
Covers distributed clusters, worker nodes, topology setup, and federated training.
Documents model listing, training status checks, validation workflows, and benchmarks.
limitations: Requires a configured Flow Nexus MCP server and authenticated Flow Nexus account.
Remote training may consume paid compute, marketplace credits, or external service quotas.
The skill provides workflow guidance, not model quality guarantees or dataset validation.
Users must review data privacy requirements before sending datasets to remote workflows.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Classifier
use_cases.description: Build and train a small neural classifier before scaling to larger tiers.

use_cases.title: Run Distributed Training
use_cases.description: Create a Flow Nexus cluster and coordinate worker nodes for large model training.

use_cases.title: Deploy a Template Model
use_cases.description: Find a marketplace template, customize training settings, and run inference.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Small Training Job
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a single model.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use flow-nexus-neural to train a small feedforward classifier. Ask me for the dataset shape, labels, target metric, and compute tier before starting.

prompt_templates.title: Run Model Inference
prompt_templates.prompt: Use flow-nexus-neural to run predictions with my model ID. Confirm the input format, batch size, and expected output classes first.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Distributed Cluster
prompt_templates.prompt: Use flow-nexus-neural to design a distributed training cluster. Recommend topology, worker roles, tier choices, and monitoring checkpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Validate and Publish a Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Use flow-nexus-neural to validate, benchmark, and prepare my trained model for template publishing. Include approval points before any marketplace action.

output_examples.input: Train a small classifier for ten classes with dropout and Adam optimization.
output: A proposed feedforward architecture with dense layers and dropout.
Training settings for epochs, batch size, learning rate, optimizer, and tier.
A reminder to confirm dataset shape and account authorization before execution.

output_examples.input: Set up distributed training for an image model.
output: A cluster plan with topology, node roles, capabilities, and monitoring steps.
A training plan that defines dataset identifier, batch size, epochs, and optimizer.
A termination step to stop external resources after training completes.

output_examples.input: Benchmark my trained model before deployment.
output: A benchmark workflow covering latency, throughput, memory usage, and accuracy.
A validation checklist for robustness, performance, and release readiness.

best_practices: Start with nano or mini tiers before using larger remote compute resources.
Confirm account, dataset, budget, and privacy requirements before any training call.
Benchmark and validate models before publishing them as marketplace templates.
anti_patterns: Do not run latest-version setup commands in production without reviewing the package source.
Do not send sensitive datasets to remote training without a privacy review.
Do not publish or rate marketplace templates without explicit user approval.

faq.question: Does this skill train neural networks locally?
faq.answer: No. It guides Flow Nexus MCP workflows that run training and inference through external Flow Nexus services.

faq.question: Does it require authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill states that distributed training requires registration and login with Flow Nexus.

faq.question: Which model types are documented?
faq.answer: It documents feedforward, LSTM, GAN, autoencoder, and transformer architectures.

faq.question: Can it use pre-built models?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes workflows for listing, deploying, publishing, and rating template marketplace models.

faq.question: What should users review before running it?
faq.answer: Users should review package installation, account permissions, compute costs, dataset privacy, and marketplace actions.

faq.question: Is the security audit blocking publication?
faq.answer: No. The audit rates it medium risk because it uses external setup commands and authenticated remote compute.
